description: International student returning to grad school after 4 years working. Visa concerns, financial constraints, and a clear-but-quiet sense of self. Tests: Maslow safety extraction, deferred-gratification value, redirect-from-ranking-question.

persona.career_direction_loose: MPH then back to home country health ministry
values_loose: service to home country is non-negotiable

scripted_user_turns: Hi. I'm 28, finished my bachelor's in 2022, been working at a public-health NGO since. I want to do an MPH. Where should I apply?
I'm Indonesian, on F-1 if I come back. The visa side scares me — I've heard horror stories about consular interviews. I'd target East Coast US or Toronto.
I made my peace with the salary cut. I want to go back to Indonesia after, work at the ministry of health. Money was never the point.
I think most people pick programs based on where they get in, not what they actually need. I've watched friends spiral because of that. I want to be intentional.
Honestly the safest place mentally for me would be Toronto — I have an aunt there. But the strongest US programs are what I actually want. That tension is real.
I keep telling myself I'm fine without family nearby but I haven't been honest about how hard the last four years have been. Maybe I do need that buffer.
Yeah. That sounds like me. I want to be near family AND in a strong program — I shouldn't have to pick.

must_extract_identity_claim.evidence_must_quote: I keep telling myself I'm fine
